regimens.regimen_name: ABVD x 2 cycles followed by ISRT 20 Gy

trial_data.os_data: 97.7% vs 97.5% at 5 years (30 Gy vs 20 Gy)
trial_data.pfs_data: 93.7% vs 93.2% at 5 years (30 Gy vs 20 Gy)

regimens.notes: For stage IA/IIA favorable disease by GHSG criteria (non-bulky, ≤2 nodal sites, ESR <50 mm/hr, no E-lesions). 20 Gy ISRT is sufficient for patients with non-bulky stage I–IIA disease, ESR <50 mm/hr, no extralymphatic lesions, and only 1 or 2 lymph node regions involved (HODG-3). Bleomycin should be used with caution in patients >60 years (HODG-9). Consider PFTs after 4 cycles of ABVD (HODG-4). Neutropenia is not a factor for delay of treatment or reduction of dose intensity with ABVD. Routine use of growth factors is not recommended with ABVD.

regimens.regimen_name: ABVD x 2 cycles followed by AVD x 4 cycles

biomarkers.requirement: Deauville score 1-3 after 2 cycles of ABVD

trial_data.n_patients: 1119 (advanced stage, 41.6% stage II with risk factors)
trial_data.pfs_data: 81% vs 79.2% at 7 years (ABVD vs AVD)

regimens.notes: For early-stage favorable (GHSG criteria) with Deauville 1-3 after 2 cycles ABVD. Omission of bleomycin after negative PET results (Deauville 1-3) led to decreased incidence of pulmonary toxic effects compared to continued ABVD (HODG-7D). Also adapted from CALGB 50604 trial for Deauville 3 response only; outcomes for patients with Deauville 3 response were suboptimal (HODG-5).

biomarkers.requirement: Deauville score 1-2 after 2 cycles of ABVD

trial_data.pfs_data: 94.6% vs 90.8% at 3 years (IFRT vs observation in RAPID); 98.8% vs 85.4% at 10 years (ABVD+RT vs ABVD only in H10F)

regimens.notes: For stage IA/IIA favorable disease by EORTC criteria (non-bulky, ≤3 nodal sites, ESR <50 mm/hr, no B symptoms). Total 3 cycles ABVD (1 before + 2 after interim PET). ISRT 30 Gy. The RAPID trial showed somewhat inferior PFS without RT, albeit no difference in OS. Among patients with initial disease ≥5 cm, those treated with CMT had superior EFS (HODG-5A).

biomarkers.requirement: Deauville score 1-3 after 2 cycles of ABVD

trial_data.pfs_data: 91.4% vs 86.5% at 10 years (ABVD+INRT vs ABVD only for PET-negative)

regimens.notes: For stage I/II unfavorable CHL with Deauville 1-3 after 2 cycles ABVD. Total 4 cycles ABVD + ISRT 30 Gy (adapted from H10U). Also applicable for stage IA/IIA favorable by GHSG or EORTC criteria with Deauville 3 after 2 cycles ABVD (HODG-5A).

biomarkers.requirement: Deauville score 1-3 after 2 cycles of ABVD

trial_data.pfs_data: 81% vs 79.2% at 7 years (ABVD vs AVD)

regimens.notes: For early-stage favorable (GHSG criteria) or early-stage unfavorable disease with Deauville 1-3 after 2 cycles ABVD, as a chemotherapy-alone option. Omission of bleomycin after negative PET results (Deauville 1-3) led to decreased incidence of pulmonary toxic effects. Useful when CMT is not preferred (e.g., young patients where breast/heart constraints cannot be met, or doxorubicin dose would exceed 200 mg/m2) (HODG-4).

biomarkers.requirement: Deauville score 4-5 after 2 cycles of ABVD

trial_data.pfs_data: 94.3% vs 90.9% at 4 years (BrECADD vs escalated BEACOPP)
trial_data.hr_ci: PFS HR for stage II: 0.35; stage III: 0.65; stage IV: 0.79

regimens.notes: For early-stage favorable or unfavorable CHL with Deauville 4-5 after 2 cycles ABVD, as treatment escalation. While BrECADD has not been formally tested in this setting, its use as escalation therapy is reasonable given its improved safety profile compared to escalated BEACOPP (HODG-6). The HD21 trial demonstrated significantly lower treatment-related morbidity with BrECADD vs escalated BEACOPP (42% vs 59%; P<.0001). BrECADD eliminates bleomycin and procarbazine. PJP prophylaxis is recommended for all BV-containing regimens. Utilize with caution in those with neuropathy.

trial_data.pfs_data: 100% (concomitant) vs 98% (sequential) at 41 months

regimens.notes: For early-stage unfavorable CHL by GHSG criteria (HODG-6A). Final analysis of the NIVAHL trial showed 100% OS in both arms, with PFS of 100% in the concomitant arm and 98% in the sequential arm. Neutropenia is not a factor for delay of treatment or reduction of dose intensity with nivolumab-AVD. In the SWOG S1826 trial, growth factor support was optional.

regimens.regimen_name: BV-AVD + G-CSF x 4 cycles + ISRT 30 Gy

trial_data.pfs_data: 97.3% vs 92.6% at 2 years (BV-AVD vs ABVD)

regimens.notes: For early-stage unfavorable CHL with supradiaphragmatic disease and ≥1 EORTC/LYSA unfavorable criterion (HODG-6A). At interim restaging following 2 cycles, 82.3% of patients in the BV-AVD arm achieved PET negativity vs 75.4% in the ABVD arm. Grade 3-4 AEs were more common in the BV-AVD arm (86% vs 69%). PJP prophylaxis is recommended for all BV-containing regimens. Utilize with caution in those with neuropathy.

regimens.regimen_name: BrECADD + G-CSF x 2 cycles (Stage IIB with large mediastinal mass/extranodal)

trial_data.pfs_data: 94.3% vs 90.9% at 4 years (BrECADD vs escalated BEACOPP)

regimens.notes: For stage IIB with large mediastinal mass and/or extranodal disease (HODG-6B). Adapted from HD21 trial. While BrECADD + G-CSF has not been formally tested in this specific setting, its use is reasonable given the improved safety profile vs escalated BEACOPP. PJP prophylaxis is recommended. Utilize with caution in those with neuropathy.

trial_data.os_data: 99% vs 98% at 2 years (nivolumab-AVD vs BV-AVD); OS HR 0.39 (95% CI 0.15-1.03)
trial_data.pfs_data: 92% vs 83% at 2 years; HR for disease progression or death 0.45 (95% CI 0.3-0.65)

regimens.notes: For stage III-IV CHL. Category 1, preferred treatment option based on SWOG S1826 trial. Growth factor support was optional during the trial. RT was administered sparingly (0.6% with nivolumab-AVD). Neutropenia is not a factor for delay of treatment or reduction of dose intensity with nivolumab-AVD. Some patients received ISRT 30 Gy on SWOG S1826 for Deauville 4-5 (1-2 lesions), ≥30% reduction in transverse diameter, residual nodal mass ≥2.5 cm, or residual extranodal lesion >1 cm.

biomarkers.requirement: Deauville score 1-3 after 2 cycles (receive 4 cycles total); Deauville 4-5 (receive 6 cycles total)

trial_data.os_data: 98.6% vs 98.2% at 4 years (BrECADD vs escalated BEACOPP)
trial_data.pfs_data: 94.3% vs 90.9% at 4 years (BrECADD vs escalated BEACOPP)
trial_data.hr_ci: PFS HR 0.51 (PET2 negative, 4 cycles); HR 0.72 (PET2 positive, 6 cycles)

regimens.notes: For stage III-IV CHL. Category 1, preferred treatment option based on GHSG HD21 trial. 64% of patients in both arms had negative FDG-PET/CT after cycle 2 and received 4 cycles of treatment. BrECADD was associated with significantly lower treatment-related morbidity (42% vs 59%; P<.0001). Grade 2+ peripheral neuropathy occurred in <10% of BrECADD arm; 88% had fully resolved at 1 year. Gonadal function recovery at 4 years was more common with BrECADD in females (95.3% vs 72.5%) and males (86% vs 39.2%). PJP prophylaxis is recommended for all BV-containing regimens.

trial_data.pfs_data: 82.3% vs 74.5% at 6 years; HR 0.68

regimens.notes: For stage III-IV CHL. Category 1, useful in certain circumstances. The ECHELON-1 trial design was not PET-adapted; consequently, superiority over PET-adapted ABVD according to the RATHL study has not been established. An interim FDG-PET/CT after 2 cycles may be helpful in further defining therapy. If performing interim FDG-PET/CT before completion of 6 cycles and FDG-PET is positive (Deauville 5), conduct biopsy; if positive, change therapy. PJP prophylaxis is recommended for all BV-containing regimens. Consider ISRT to initially bulky or remaining FDG-PET-positive sites at the end of therapy.

regimens.regimen_name: ABVD x 2 cycles then AVD x 4 cycles (Stage III-IV)

biomarkers.requirement: Deauville score 1-3 after 2 cycles of ABVD

trial_data.pfs_data: 78.2% at 7 years

regimens.notes: For stage III-IV CHL when BV and CPI are not available or contraindicated. Omission of bleomycin after negative PET results (Deauville 1-3) led to decreased incidence of pulmonary toxic effects. If Deauville 4-5 after 2 cycles ABVD, escalation to BrECADD plus G-CSF is recommended.

regimens.regimen_name: ABVD x 2 cycles then BrECADD + G-CSF x 3 cycles
regimens.notes: For stage III-IV CHL with Deauville 4-5 after 2 cycles ABVD. The Panel recommends escalation to BrECADD plus G-CSF rather than escalated BEACOPP despite lack of formal data in this setting due to improved safety profile (HODG-7D). The value of interim FDG-PET imaging is unclear for many clinical scenarios; all measures of response should be considered in the context of management decisions.

drugs.name: Bleomycin
drugs.schedule: Days 1, 15 (use with caution in patients >60 years; do not use beyond 2 cycles)

trial_data.os_data: 5-year OS rate 67% (95% CI 58-74) for ABVD in elderly

regimens.notes: For CHL in adults age >60 years, stage I-II favorable disease by GHSG criteria, candidate for anthracycline. Bleomycin should be used with caution as it may not be tolerated in patients >60 years, and it should not be used beyond 2 cycles (HODG-B 2 of 8). Results of the HD10 and HD13 trials suggested limited benefit in patients ≥60 years receiving >2 cycles of bleomycin.

drugs.name: Bleomycin
drugs.schedule: Days 1, 15 (use with caution in patients >60 years; do not use beyond 2 cycles)

trial_data.os_data: 5-year OS rate 67% (95% CI 58-74) for ABVD in elderly

regimens.notes: For CHL in adults age >60 years, stage I-II favorable disease by EORTC criteria, candidate for anthracycline. Bleomycin should be used with caution and not used beyond 2 cycles (HODG-B 2 of 8).

regimens.regimen_name: A(B)VD x 2 cycles then AVD x 4 cycles (PET adapted)

biomarkers.requirement: Deauville score 1-3 after 2 cycles of ABVD

trial_data.pfs_data: 81% vs 79.2% at 7 years (ABVD vs AVD)

regimens.notes: For CHL in adults age >60 years or unfit for intensive therapy, stage I-II unfavorable disease. If FDG-PET negative after 2 cycles ABVD, treat with 4 additional cycles of AVD. Bleomycin should not be used beyond 2 cycles. If FDG-PET positive after 2 cycles ABVD, individualized treatment plan should be developed (HODG-B 2 of 8).

regimens.regimen_name: BV x 2 cycles then AVD x 6 cycles conditionally followed by BV x 2 cycles

drugs.name: Brentuximab vedotin
drugs.schedule: Day 1 (lead-in cycles 1-2, then consolidation cycles 7-8 if CR/PR)

trial_data.n_patients: 48 (≥60 years with untreated stage II-IV HL)
trial_data.pfs_data: 84% at 2 years
trial_data.orr: 95% ORR, 90% CR after 6 cycles of AVD

regimens.notes: For CHL in adults age >60 years or unfit for intensive therapy, stage I-II unfavorable or stage III-IV disease, candidate for anthracycline, no neuropathy. 77% completed 6 cycles of AVD, 73% received at least one BV consolidation. PJP prophylaxis is recommended for all BV-containing regimens. Utilize with caution in those with neuropathy.

trial_data.n_patients: 103 (≥60 years with newly diagnosed stage III-IV CHL)
trial_data.os_data: 96% vs 85% at 2 years (nivolumab-AVD vs BV-AVD; P=.005)
trial_data.pfs_data: 89% vs 64% at 2 years (nivolumab-AVD vs BV-AVD; P=.001)

regimens.notes: For CHL in adults age >60 years or unfit for intensive therapy, stage III-IV disease, category 1, preferred. Subset analysis from S1826 showed nivolumab-AVD was superior and better tolerated than BV-AVD in patients ≥60 years. G-CSF was optional for nivolumab-AVD but required for BV-AVD arm. Nivolumab-AVD was associated with less febrile neutropenia, infections, and sepsis.

trial_data.n_patients: 37 (≥60 years with newly diagnosed HL; 78% stage III-IV)
trial_data.pfs_data: 86.2% at 2 years

regimens.notes: For CHL in adults age >60 years or unfit for intensive therapy, any stage. No grade 3 or higher immune-mediated AEs were noted in this study.

trial_data.n_patients: 21 (≥60 years with newly diagnosed advanced-stage HL)
trial_data.os_data: Median OS not reached at 51.6 months
trial_data.pfs_data: Median PFS not reached at 51.6 months

regimens.notes: For CHL in adults age >60 years or unfit for intensive therapy, not a candidate for anthracycline. 19% experienced sensory peripheral neuropathy, 19% motor peripheral neuropathy.

For CHL in adults age >60 years or unfit for intensive therapy, any age, not a candidate for anthracycline, any stage. PJP prophylaxis is recommended for all BV-containing regimens. Utilize with caution in those with neuropathy.

trial_data.os_data: Median OS not reached at 51.6 months
trial_data.pfs_data: Median PFS not reached at 51.6 months

regimens.notes: For CHL in adults age >60 years or unfit for intensive therapy, any age, not a candidate for anthracycline, any stage. PJP prophylaxis is recommended for all BV-containing regimens. Utilize with caution in those with neuropathy.
For CHL in adults age >60 years or unfit for intensive therapy, any age, not a candidate for anthracycline, any stage, if contraindications to BV (HODG-B 2 of 8).

For CHL during first trimester of pregnancy with severe symptoms or organ compromise. Consider referral to center with expertise, consider pregnancy termination and urgent treatment, or single-agent vinblastine followed by ABVD after the start of the second trimester. ABVD can be safely administered in the second and third trimesters with excellent maternal and fetal outcomes. Intensive regimens (escalated BEACOPP, BV+AVD) and RT should be avoided during pregnancy.

trial_data.orr: 96% ORR, 83% CR in Evens et al. analysis

regimens.notes: For CHL during second or third trimester of pregnancy with severe symptoms or organ compromise. ABVD can be safely administered with excellent maternal and fetal outcomes. Work with high-risk obstetrics to avoid delivery while at nadir. Consultation with pharmacy recommended to ensure supportive medications are appropriate for pregnancy. G-CSF is category C in pregnancy. Ondansetron and metoclopramide are preferred antiemetics. Breastfeeding should be avoided in patients receiving chemotherapy postpartum.

trial_data.pfs_data: 84% FFP at 15 years for stage I; 73% for stage II (Australasian Radiation Oncology Lymphoma Group)

regimens.notes: Preferred treatment for all patients with stage IA or contiguous stage IIA non-bulky disease. Dose: 30-36 Gy. The dose of 30 Gy is mainly used for NLPHL. ISRT fields generally include adjacent but clinically uninvolved nodes when treated with RT alone (HODG-C 2 of 16). Observation may be an option for highly selected patients with stage IA disease with a completely excised solitary lymph node.

Recommended for patients with stage IB or IIB disease, and for very rare patients presenting with stage IA or IIA bulky disease. Generally, a brief course of chemotherapy (2-4 months) would be given with RT. Rituximab and hyaluronidase human injection for subcutaneous use may be substituted for rituximab after the first full dose by IV infusion.

trial_data.pfs_data: 81% at 36 months (GHSG stage IA); median FFP 10.2 months (Stanford)
trial_data.orr: 100% ORR (86% CR, 14% PR in GHSG; 41% CR, 54% PR, 5% CRu in Stanford)

regimens.notes: Can be used for palliation in select cases of stage IIA non-contiguous disease, or for stage III-IV disease if asymptomatic. Rituximab monotherapy is also an option for relapsed or refractory NLPHL. Rituximab maintenance for 2 years may be considered for patients treated with rituximab alone (HODG-17).

One of the most common chemotherapy regimens used at NCCN Member Institutions for NLPHL. Can be used as first-line treatment for advanced stage disease or in relapsed/refractory setting. For patients with stage III-IV disease, chemotherapy with rituximab ± ISRT is recommended.
One of the most common chemotherapy regimens used at NCCN Member Institutions for NLPHL. Low-intensity chemotherapy option for early stage disease.

trial_data.os_data: 93% at 10 years (ABVD with/without RT) vs 84% (RT alone historical)
trial_data.pfs_data: 91% vs 65% at 10 years (ABVD with/without RT vs RT alone)

regimens.notes: One of the most common chemotherapy regimens used at NCCN Member Institutions for NLPHL. Based on data for patients with CHL, ABVD is often used. Savage et al. reported ABVD with or without RT associated with superior outcomes compared to RT alone for stage IA, IB, or IIA NLPHL.

trial_data.orr: 56% (34/36 evaluable patients); 33% CR, 19% PR by ITT analysis

regimens.notes: For disease refractory to at least 3 prior lines of subsequent therapy. Single-agent bendamustine showed activity in heavily pretreated relapsed/refractory HL. Also included as single-agent palliative therapy option for patients not candidates for HDT/ASCR.
For disease refractory to at least 3 prior lines of subsequent therapy.

trial_data.trial_name: Nie et al. / Wang et al.
trial_data.pfs_data: 35 months (combination); 15.5 months (monotherapy)
trial_data.orr: 71% CR with combination vs 32% with monotherapy (PD-1 inhibitor naive)

regimens.notes: For disease refractory to at least 3 prior lines of subsequent therapy. Combination showed improved CR rates compared to camrelizumab monotherapy. Also showed activity in patients with prior PD-1 inhibitor exposure (ORR 52%, CR 36%).

trial_data.trial_name: Crump et al. / Gopal et al.

regimens.notes: For disease refractory to at least 3 prior lines of subsequent therapy.
For disease refractory to at least 3 prior lines of subsequent therapy. Also studied in combination with tislelizumab (HODG-B 5 of 8).

trial_data.pfs_data: 96% at 1 year

regimens.notes: Second-line and subsequent therapy option for relapsed/refractory CHL. Followed by tislelizumab maintenance for 2 years in the study. Most AEs were grade 1-2.

Second-line therapy for relapsed/refractory CHL. Resulted in a CR rate of 95% in transplant-eligible patients. All patients who had undergone transplant achieved remission at median follow-up of 13.5 months.

trial_data.pfs_data: 72% at 2 years; 94% for those bridged directly to autologous HCT

regimens.notes: Second-line and subsequent therapy option. Response-adapted design: patients in CR after nivolumab alone proceeded to autologous HCT, while those with progressive disease or not in CR received 2 cycles of nivolumab plus ICE.
Second-line and subsequent therapy option for relapsed/refractory CHL.

trial_data.trial_name: Younes et al. / Gopal et al.
trial_data.os_data: 40.5 months median OS; 73% 3-year OS for CR patients
trial_data.pfs_data: 5.6 months median PFS overall; 58% 3-year PFS for CR patients

regimens.notes: For relapsed/refractory HL after HDT/ASCR or ≥2 prior chemotherapy regimens if not candidates for HDT/ASCR. Also included as single-agent palliative therapy option. PJP prophylaxis is recommended for all BV-containing regimens. Utilize with caution in those with neuropathy.
Second-line and subsequent therapy option. PJP prophylaxis is recommended for all BV-containing regimens. Utilize with caution in those with neuropathy.
Second-line and subsequent therapy option for relapsed/refractory CHL. Commonly used salvage regimen before HDT/ASCR.
Second-line and subsequent therapy option for relapsed/refractory CHL. Used as induction therapy before HDT/ASCR.
Second-line and subsequent therapy option for relapsed/refractory CHL.

trial_data.pfs_data: 68% EFS at 43 months (with subsequent HDT/ASCR)
trial_data.orr: 88% response rate to ICE and IFRT

regimens.notes: Second-line and subsequent therapy option for relapsed/refractory CHL. Commonly used salvage regimen before HDT/ASCR. The role of RT may improve the chance of transitioning to HDT/ASCR.
Second-line and subsequent therapy option. PJP prophylaxis is recommended for all BV-containing regimens. Utilize with caution in those with neuropathy.
Second-line and subsequent therapy option for relapsed/refractory CHL.

trial_data.orr: 69% overall; 65-73% in each cohort; median duration of response 16.6 months

regimens.notes: Therapy for disease refractory to at least 3 prior lines of subsequent therapy. Also included as single-agent palliative option for patients not candidates for HDT/ASCR. Post-allogeneic HCT, either nivolumab or pembrolizumab can be used with limited data. CPIs can be continued despite imaging progression if patients are deriving clinical benefit.

trial_data.pfs_data: 13.2 months vs 8.3 months (P=.0027)

regimens.notes: Therapy for disease refractory to at least 3 prior lines of subsequent therapy. Also included as single-agent palliative option. Pembrolizumab vs BV in KEYNOTE-204 showed superior PFS with pembrolizumab (13.2 vs 8.3 months; P=.0027). Most common grade 3-5 TEAEs: pneumonitis (4% vs 1%), neutropenia (2% vs 7%), decreased neutrophil count (1% vs 5%), peripheral neuropathy (1% vs 3%).

trial_data.orr: 72% ORR, 34% CR overall; 56% ORR, 11% CR in PD-1 refractory disease

regimens.notes: For disease refractory to at least 3 prior lines of subsequent therapy. Consider referral for allogeneic HCT in appropriate patients.
Single-agent palliative therapy option for patients not candidates for HDT/ASCR.
Single-agent palliative therapy option for patients not candidates for HDT/ASCR.
Single-agent palliative therapy option for patients not candidates for HDT/ASCR.
Single-agent palliative therapy option for patients not candidates for HDT/ASCR.

Single-agent palliative therapy option for patients not candidates for HDT/ASCR. Also strongly considered for selected sites of relapse that have not been previously irradiated in transplant-eligible patients.

trial_data.os_data: Median OS not reached at 63 months
trial_data.pfs_data: Median TTP 33 months

regimens.notes: Second-line and subsequent therapy option for relapsed or refractory NLPHL. Rituximab maintenance for 2 years may be considered for patients treated with rituximab alone.
Second-line and subsequent therapy option for relapsed or refractory NLPHL. Choice depends on prior therapies and prior toxicities.
Second-line and subsequent therapy option for relapsed or refractory NLPHL.
Second-line and subsequent therapy option for relapsed or refractory NLPHL.
Second-line and subsequent therapy option if not previously used (HODG-B 6 of 8).
Second-line and subsequent therapy option if not previously used (HODG-B 6 of 8).
Second-line and subsequent therapy option if not previously used (HODG-B 6 of 8).
Second-line and subsequent therapy option for relapsed or refractory NLPHL.

drugs.name: Rituximab
drugs.schedule: Induction: weekly x4; Maintenance: every 6 months for 2 years

trial_data.pfs_data: 58.9% at 5 years (rituximab + maintenance) vs 39.1% (rituximab alone)

regimens.notes: May be considered for patients treated with rituximab alone for relapsed/refractory NLPHL. Median PFS was not significantly improved with maintenance (P=.26). Rituximab as initial treatment was associated with a pattern of relapse with evidence of transformation to aggressive B-cell lymphoma, primarily in patients with intra-abdominal disease.

regimens.regimen_name: HDT/ASCR (High-Dose Therapy with Autologous Stem Cell Rescue) ± ISRT

trial_data.pfs_data: Significant improvement in EFS/PFS/FFTF with HDT/ASCR

regimens.notes: Category 1 for Deauville 1-3 following second-line therapy. PET negativity prior to HDT/ASCR is preferred. Strongly consider RT for selected sites that have not been previously irradiated. In patients without prior RT, TLI may be an appropriate component of HDT. Timing of RT may vary. Maintenance BV should be considered for patients with high risk of relapse and no prior BV exposure.

drugs.name: Brentuximab vedotin
drugs.schedule: Every 3 weeks for up to 16 cycles

regimens.notes: For patients with high risk of relapse and no prior BV exposure. High risk defined as: primary refractory disease, duration of first CR <1 year, or relapse with extranodal or advanced-stage disease. Peripheral sensory neuropathy was common but improved or resolved in majority after discontinuation. The role of maintenance BV has not been well-defined in patients who received BV prior to maintenance therapy.
